skill.name: motion-graphics
skill.description: Create short, design-led HyperFrames motion graphics such as kinetic type, stat hits, chart moments, logo stings, lower thirds, animated social posts, webpage highlights, and map shots.
skill.summary: Build short, motion-first HyperFrames compositions with reusable blocks, asset sourcing guidance, validation, and render steps.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported a high volume of command, network, filesystem, and environment findings. Most Markdown backtick and weak-crypto alerts are false positives from documentation, examples, and animation terminology, but two helper scripts do execute local tools and can contact third-party services. No prompt injection attempt or malicious intent was found, but publication should include warnings for script execution, external asset fetching, and optional image upload to a Google API.

medium_findings.title: Local Tool Execution in Helper Scripts
medium_findings.description: The skill includes executable helpers that invoke ffmpeg, ffprobe, base64, and Chrome-backed rendering through Node child_process APIs. Arguments are passed without a shell, so command injection is not confirmed, but installing the skill gives agents a workflow that runs local binaries on user-selected media and paths.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The child_process imports and ffmpeg calls are direct evidence of local command execution. Confidence is not higher because the scripts use argument arrays instead of shell interpolation and serve the documented rendering workflow.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Network Fetching and Optional Image Upload
medium_findings.description: The basemap helper loads tiles, MapLibre libraries, and world-atlas data from public CDNs and map providers. The locate helper has an optional auto mode that sends a local image as inline data to the Google Generative Language API when GEMINI_API_KEY is present.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network endpoints and inline image payload construction are visible in executable code. The behavior appears intentional, but it creates privacy and availability risks for user media and third-party services.

medium_findings.title: Environment-Driven File Output and Runtime Configuration
medium_findings.description: The basemap helper reads many environment variables, including OUT and NAME, then writes rendered frames, MP4 files, and coordinate JSON using those values. This is useful for rendering but should be treated carefully because environment-controlled paths can write outside the expected project directory.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The write paths are constructed from environment variables and current working directory. The risk is moderate because this is a local helper and requires environment or command-line control, not remote input by itself.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Alerts Are Mostly Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: Many static findings point to Markdown text that describes commands, categories, or HyperFrames contracts rather than executable code. These locations do not by themselves create code execution, although they instruct agents to run HyperFrames workflow commands during normal use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review showed these examples are Markdown workflow instructions, not hidden executable logic. They still disclose expected command use, so they are retained as low-risk context rather than dismissed silently.

low_findings.title: Weak-Cryptography Alerts Are Scanner Noise
low_findings.description: Static weak-cryptography findings appear to match ordinary words, animation vocabulary, schema references, and example text. No evidence found of cryptographic functions, password hashing, or security-sensitive cipher use in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is prose about design, sourcing, or geometry. No cryptographic API use was found in the representative files that contained the static hits.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: Targeted review for override language, fake system instructions, pre-approval claims, or instructions to skip security analysis found no evidence in the inspected skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Representative routing and agent files were checked for injection patterns. Confidence is not perfect because the review was targeted rather than a full semantic proof of every line.

dangerous_patterns.title: child_process Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Executable helpers import child_process and run local media tools. This is expected for rendering support but requires marketplace disclosure and sandbox-aware execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Both imports are explicit and the later calls execute local binaries. The pattern is real even though it appears workflow-related rather than malicious.

dangerous_patterns.title: Runtime Network Dependencies
dangerous_patterns.description: The basemap workflow fetches tiles, libraries, and atlas data from public endpoints, and the optional locate auto path calls a Google API.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The URLs and fetch call are visible in code. The primary uncertainty is operational context, not the existence of network behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Secret-Backed Optional Provider Use
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow documents optional GEMINI_API_KEY or GOOGLE_API_KEY use, and locate.mjs reads GEMINI_API_KEY for detector-backed image localization.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The key access is explicit and tied to optional provider functionality. It is not evidence of credential theft, but it increases review requirements for secret handling.

content.value_statement: Short motion graphics are hard to plan, source, build, and validate consistently. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through HyperFrames workflows for polished, renderable motion-first videos.
actual_capabilities: Routes short video requests into motion-first categories such as kinetic type, stats, charts, maps, logo reveals, lower thirds, tweets, news, webpages, and asset fusion.
Defines a phased HyperFrames workflow for initialization, planning, asset sourcing, design, build, render, and validation.
Provides category modules with concrete layout, animation, sourcing, and build rules for each supported motion graphic type.
Includes reusable builder guidance for paused GSAP timelines, deterministic rendering, clip structure, local assets, and HyperFrames validation.
Ships helper scripts for map basemap baking and visual target localization when advanced asset-aligned graphics are needed.
Supports MP4 output and alpha overlay output through HyperFrames render formats.
limitations: It is intended for short, unnarrated motion graphics, usually under 30 seconds.
Long narrated videos, product promos, website tours, PR explainers, and captioning workflows should use other HyperFrames skills.
Some categories depend on external search, capture, map tiles, image generation, or provider keys.
Helper scripts require local tools such as Node, ffmpeg, Chrome, and HyperFrames.

use_cases.title: Launch a Social Motion Hit
use_cases.description: Turn a short claim, stat, quote, or headline into a polished motion-first clip for social channels.

use_cases.title: Animate Product Interface Highlights
use_cases.description: Capture a webpage or interface and create a quick visual highlight with scroll, cursor, zoom, and callout motion.

use_cases.title: Create Broadcast-Style Data Moments
use_cases.description: Build charts, stat reveals, or map shots that use focused animation to make one data point clear.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: Kinetic Text Clip
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a quick typography animation from supplied copy.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 6 second vertical motion graphic with the line: "Ship the moment, not the meeting." Use kinetic type, high contrast, and MP4 output.

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate: Stat Reveal
prompt_templates.scenario: Animate one number with supporting context.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an 8 second stat motion graphic for 73 percent adoption. Use a count-up, a compact subtitle, and a clean data-focused visual style.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced: Webpage Highlight
prompt_templates.scenario: Capture and animate a real page region.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a short webpage highlight from this URL. Capture the page, zoom to the pricing section, add a cursor click, and end on one callout.

prompt_templates.title: Expert: Map Basemap Shot
prompt_templates.scenario: Use map baking and overlays for a geographic motion graphic.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 10 second 16:9 map shot that zooms to France and Germany, bakes a dark basemap, draws borders, adds labels, and renders an MP4.

output_examples.input: A founder wants a 5 second quote animation for a launch post.
output_examples.output: The skill plans a kinetic-type shot, selects typography motion, builds a HyperFrames composition, validates it, and renders a short MP4.

output_examples.input: A marketing team wants a quick webpage feature highlight.
output_examples.output: The skill captures the page, freezes local assets, designs a scroll and zoom sequence, adds cursor and callout motion, then renders the final clip.

output_examples.input: A newsroom wants a map graphic showing two highlighted countries.
output_examples.output: The skill chooses the map category, bakes a deterministic basemap when needed, aligns SVG overlays, adds attribution, and renders the video.

best_practices: Keep the message to one dominant motion idea and one clear viewer takeaway.
Use project-local frozen assets and validate with HyperFrames lint and inspect before rendering.
Choose asset-free categories when external search or provider keys are unavailable.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for long narrated videos or multi-scene brand reels.
Do not rely on live render-time network assets when deterministic output is required.
Do not skip visual validation after building or repairing a composition.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates short HyperFrames motion graphics such as kinetic type, stats, charts, logo reveals, webpage highlights, maps, and overlays.

faq.question: Can it make narrated videos?
faq.answer: No. It is designed for short, unnarrated motion-first pieces. Use a general or explainer video workflow for narration.

faq.question: Does it need external services?
faq.answer: Some categories can use search, capture, image generation, map tiles, or provider keys. Asset-free categories can work without those services.

faq.question: What tools does it expect locally?
faq.answer: It expects HyperFrames, Node, ffmpeg, and sometimes Chrome for rendering, inspection, map baking, or image localization.

faq.question: Can it export transparent overlays?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow supports alpha overlay exports through HyperFrames formats such as WebM or MOV.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with disclosure. The skill is medium risk because helper scripts run local tools and can use network services, but no malicious intent was found.
